title: Fish Stuck

jokes.text: So, I needed a new lawyer, and Susie was going on and on about what a great lawyer you are. And then, the coup de grâce, she told me your last name. - Oh. - Mantle. Christopher Mantle.
jokes.setup: Larry explains how he found his new lawyer through Susie's recommendation
jokes.punchline: The dramatic revelation that the lawyer's name is 'Mantle' like Mickey Mantle
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry setup - his obsession with names becomes a plot driver. Well-structured reveal with Christopher's awkward reaction.

jokes.text: I mean, Mickey Mantle, he was my idol growing up. I don't follow baseball, but I know who Mickey Mantle is.
jokes.setup: Larry claims Mickey Mantle was his idol
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by saying he doesn't follow baseball
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David contradiction - enthusiastic about something he admits knowing nothing about.

jokes.text: We're both being dragged. - Look at that.
jokes.setup: Both Larry and Christopher are reluctantly attending the temple event
jokes.punchline: Larry's pleased recognition of their shared misery
jokes.explanation: Simple bonding moment over shared reluctance. Economy is good but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Mr. Mantle. - Yeah. All... You... You just hit a home run. - You know? - That's fantastic.
jokes.setup: Christopher delivers good legal news about Larry's case
jokes.punchline: Larry forces an awkward baseball pun because of the lawyer's name
jokes.explanation: Larry can't resist the obvious pun, creating perfect cringe. Christopher's discomfort makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She took an IQ test? - I mean, you don't wanna get a moron, right?
jokes.setup: Christopher mentions his surrogate
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate concern about the surrogate's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate question. His casual use of 'moron' while trying to be helpful is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: You're going with Zeckelman over Mantle?
jokes.setup: Christopher reveals they're choosing his husband's last name 'Zeckelman'
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction to choosing Zeckelman over the 'superior' Mantle
jokes.explanation: Larry's obsession with names reaches peak absurdity. His genuine bewilderment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I don't know any Zeckelmans who hit 536 home runs. - Yet.
jokes.setup: Larry criticizes the Zeckelman name's lack of baseball achievement
jokes.punchline: Christopher's dry 'Yet' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. One word that completely deflates Larry's argument while maintaining dignity. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Tinkerbell flies her little ass in there. She put her little booty out, with that little booty tooch. And bling!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Freddy's new girlfriend who works at Disney
jokes.punchline: Freddy's graphic sexual description of cartoon Tinkerbell
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd sexual fantasy about a cartoon character. Freddy's enthusiasm makes it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: It speaks of a very unusual perversion. A man attracted to a thimble-sized fairy.
jokes.setup: Freddy's Tinkerbell fantasy
jokes.punchline: Larry's clinical analysis of the perversion
jokes.explanation: Larry's pseudo-intellectual response to sexual absurdity. The phrase 'thimble-sized fairy' is perfectly specific and funny.

jokes.text: I'll put her in my motherfucking pocket. You hear me? And I'll just walk around knowing she home.
jokes.setup: Continued Tinkerbell fantasy
jokes.punchline: Freddy's domestic fantasy about pocket-dwelling Tinkerbell
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity with domestic bliss fantasy. 'knowing she home' is perfectly weird phrasing.

jokes.text: Fish stuck. - Fish sick. - Fish stuck. - Fish sick.
jokes.setup: Larry notices a fish that appears trapped in the aquarium filter
jokes.punchline: Stubborn disagreement over whether fish is stuck or sick
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsession with being right. The repetitive argument structure builds absurd tension.

jokes.text: Fish don't sneeze. - No, fish sneeze.
jokes.setup: Waiter claims fish is sneezing
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical objection vs waiter's insistence
jokes.explanation: Absurd biological debate. Larry's certainty meets equally stubborn nonsense.

jokes.text: At least that motherfucker can't drown.
jokes.setup: Larry is upset about the stuck/sick fish
jokes.punchline: Freddy's darkly optimistic observation about fish biology
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor punctuation to the fish debate. Excellent economy and unexpected perspective.

jokes.text: Tell ya what, you alcoholics, you know your way around a good donut.
jokes.setup: Larry at AA meeting with good donuts
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate generalization about alcoholics and donuts
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate compliment. He means well but says exactly the wrong thing.

jokes.text: You know, a lot of people find it funny, but he works so blue. I heard the word 'hell' like... Three times.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Richard Lewis's AA sharing
jokes.punchline: Cyrus considers saying 'hell' to be working blue
jokes.explanation: Gentle mockery of over-sensitivity. The idea that 'hell' is scandalously blue is endearingly naive.

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing, man? Hell away from me. - He hates being hugged.
jokes.setup: Someone tries to hug Richard Lewis
jokes.punchline: Lewis's violent rejection of physical contact
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from Lewis's neurosis. His use of 'hell' right after the blue material discussion adds irony.

jokes.text: There's no touching here. No touching.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to hug Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's panicked rejection of physical affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry physical comedy - his horror at unwanted human contact

jokes.text: I thought you were supposed to share here. It sounds like you're doing stand-up.
jokes.setup: Richard Lewis gets big laughs at AA meeting
jokes.punchline: Larry points out Lewis is essentially doing comedy instead of therapy
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on comedians using personal pain for material. Larry cuts through the pretense.

jokes.text: Well, there was a sad old lady who opened for me, but she only did ten minutes.
jokes.setup: Larry questions if Lewis lets others talk
jokes.punchline: Lewis describes other AA members in show business terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy. Lewis has completely reframed AA as a comedy venue with himself as headliner. 'Opened for me' is perfect.

jokes.text: The only problem is you're doing your act, but nobody's drinking. Imagine if they were drinking. The roof would be blown off.
jokes.setup: Lewis killing at AA meetings
jokes.punchline: Larry imagines how much funnier Lewis would be to drunk audiences
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical extension of the premise. The irony of a comedian needing sober audiences is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: The AA special. This is the hook I've been looking for. Why not talk about being sober but get a special with HBO?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lewis's AA meeting success
jokes.punchline: Lewis immediately sees it as a career opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lewis character moment - turning personal recovery into career advancement. Very meta for HBO show.

jokes.text: First of all, I hate asking for favors almost as much as I hate doing favors.
jokes.setup: Larry needs to ask Jenna for a favor
jokes.punchline: His honest admission of hating both sides of the favor equation
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry David honesty. Most people hide their reluctance; Larry leads with it.

jokes.text: Where did you find her? Point to the planet. There's no more. There's no more of 'em.
jokes.setup: Jenna agrees to read the script enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Freddy's over-the-top amazement at finding someone helpful
jokes.explanation: Freddy's exaggerated romantic enthusiasm. The alien planet metaphor works for his amazement.

jokes.text: I assume it's your semen, right? 'Cause that's how you got Zeckelman?
jokes.setup: Larry questions how they chose the baby's last name
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate assumption about whose genetic material is being used
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness. He treats intimate details like logical data points to analyze.

jokes.text: Zeckelman needs a cool first name!
jokes.setup: The couple tries to leave as Larry keeps suggesting names
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate justification for his interference
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete inability to let go of the name issue. His logic is sound but his persistence is insane.

jokes.text: Doc Zeckelman is a joke. - Ace. - Ace Zeckelman? That's not a great name?
jokes.setup: Larry continues suggesting names as they walk away
jokes.punchline: Christopher's exasperated dismissal vs Larry's genuine belief in 'Ace Zeckelman'
jokes.explanation: The escalating absurdity as Larry won't stop. 'Ace Zeckelman' does have a certain ridiculous charm.

jokes.text: Dewey, Duncan, Deegan. Deegan. After the Major Deegan Expressway.
jokes.setup: More name suggestions
jokes.punchline: Naming a child after a highway
jokes.explanation: Absurd naming logic - highway as inspiration for child's name

jokes.text: The fuck?
jokes.setup: Someone witnesses the name argument aftermath
jokes.punchline: Pure bewildered reaction to the scene Larry just created
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat punctuating the chaos Larry created. Good economy but minimal craft.

jokes.text: This is a shanda.
jokes.setup: Larry complains to the Rabbi about the defamatory brick
jokes.punchline: Larry uses Yiddish to emphasize his outrage
jokes.explanation: Larry deploying his limited Yiddish for maximum dramatic effect. Solid character moment.

jokes.text: There's nothing in the Old Testament about defamatory bricks? You think if Isaac chiseled something defamatory about Jedediah, you think it wouldn't be taken down?
jokes.setup: Arguing with rabbi about brick policy
jokes.punchline: Larry's biblical legal precedent argument
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd legal argument using biblical precedent for brick disputes

jokes.text: You're joining the team? Are you prepared to complain a lot?
jokes.setup: Learning about conversion to Judaism
jokes.punchline: Larry's essential qualification for being Jewish
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about Jewish stereotypes from an insider perspective

jokes.text: Can't deal with the alternative.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about obvious reasons for converting to Judaism
jokes.punchline: Christopher's dark implication about avoiding hell
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny theological insurance policy. Great economy in the phrasing.

jokes.text: I gotta schmeitz. - You what? - Schmeitz. - What's that one? - Gotta go. - That's a new one to me.
jokes.setup: Christopher tries to end conversation politely
jokes.punchline: He makes up a Yiddish word that doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Christopher's fake Yiddish to seem authentic backfires. Larry's genuine confusion makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm a greeter, and I talk to the guests. I put on a costume, like, a princess, like. Snow White or Elsa or Ariel or Lilo or Stitch. My favorite is Mickey's favorite. Minnie Mouse.
jokes.setup: Larry assumed Jenna was a Disney executive
jokes.punchline: She's actually a costume character at a mall Disney store
jokes.explanation: Major misdirection payoff. The audience shares Freddy's shock at the reality vs expectation.

jokes.text: Oh, boy.
jokes.setup: Learning Jenna works at the Disney store, not Disney corporation
jokes.punchline: Freddy's devastated understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflated reaction. Two words that convey complete romantic devastation.

jokes.text: I'm so superficial, I'd probably break up. - You would? Right away? - Yeah. I'm not gonna date Minnie Mouse.
jokes.setup: Freddy asks what Larry would do about the Jenna situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about his shallowness
jokes.explanation: Larry's shocking self-awareness and honesty. 'I'm not gonna date Minnie Mouse' is perfectly blunt.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, I'm gonna listen to the notes from Minnie Mouse!
jokes.setup: Remembering he gave Jenna a script to read
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of taking professional advice from someone in costume
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the implications. The professional/costume character divide is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, oh, my God. We're coming out with a new line of Little Mermaid sleepwear, and I get a discount, so if you text me your sizes, I can hook you up.
jokes.setup: Jenna finishing her script notes to Dana
jokes.punchline: She offers Little Mermaid pajamas as if it's a major perk
jokes.explanation: Perfect punctuation to the script meeting. The disconnect between professional consultation and pajama discounts.

jokes.text: Jenna giving detailed, helpful script notes despite her actual job
jokes.setup: Jenna reviews the script professionally
jokes.punchline: Her insightful feedback contrasts with her day job
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectations about competence. Shows Larry's prejudices are unfounded.

jokes.text: It's the 'I like it' gambit. All you gotta do is compliment people on what the fuck they're wearing, next thing you know, they gonna give you one.
jokes.setup: Larry gets given a jacket after complimenting it
jokes.punchline: Freddy identifies this as a reproducible social hack
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social observation codified into a 'gambit.' Freddy's analytical approach to social manipulation is funny.

jokes.text: Larry David is disrespectful to women. Come on, Cheryl. I love women. Who loves women more than me? I love having sex with them.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about defamatory brick
jokes.punchline: His defense immediately proves the brick's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-sabotage. He equates loving sex with respecting women, proving the brick's accuracy.

jokes.text: You bought a brick, huh? - Yes. - What does your brick say, Ted? - Nothing. We left it blank.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Ted and Cheryl bought a brick
jokes.punchline: They left their brick completely blank
jokes.explanation: The blank brick is perfect passive-aggressive charity. They fulfilled obligation while saying nothing.

jokes.text: All right, so just for argument's sake, is there anything nice you could say about me that's not on a brick?
jokes.setup: Cheryl won't write anything nice on a brick
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate lowering of standards to non-brick compliments
jokes.explanation: Larry's pathetic attempt to salvage any compliment. The arbitrary brick restriction becomes the focus.

jokes.text: Any woman who Larry David impregnated, he not only paid for the abortion, but accompanied her to the procedure.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests brick text about supporting women
jokes.punchline: His idea of feminist credentials is abortion accompaniment
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate defense that reveals more than it defends. Larry's logic is simultaneously thoughtful and horrifying.

jokes.text: Fish sad, so we changed new location. Change of scenery make much happy fish. - So, you moved the fish? - Fish living best life in downtown.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about the stuck fish from earlier
jokes.punchline: Waiter's elaborate fiction about fish relocation
jokes.explanation: Great callback with escalated absurdity. 'Living best life in downtown' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Stuck, dead! Stuck, dead! - Sick, gone! Sick, gone!
jokes.setup: Continuing fish debate
jokes.punchline: The argument devolving into chanted opposition
jokes.explanation: Escalation of linguistic conflict into rhythmic chanting

jokes.text: I will not forget fish.
jokes.setup: Waiter insists fish is fine after elaborate relocation story
jokes.punchline: Larry's solemn vow to remember the fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession with justice. His memorial vow for a fish is both touching and absurd.

jokes.text: Tell me you got something good. - Oh, I got the goods. Got something that's gonna get us out of this mess that we're both in.
jokes.setup: Both Larry and Freddy need to escape their relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Freddy promises a solution to both their problems
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the big scheme. Freddy's confidence promises something elaborate.

jokes.text: It's called, uh, Groat's disease. I have Groat's disease. It affects the nervous system and eventually I become incontinent, and you can't even sit.
jokes.setup: Freddy reveals his escape plan
jokes.punchline: He's faking a horrible degenerative disease
jokes.explanation: Elaborate medical fiction for relationship escape. The specific symptoms make it more believable and funnier.

jokes.text: Freddy's fake Groat's disease performance with graphic symptoms
jokes.setup: Freddy pretends to have degenerative disease
jokes.punchline: Elaborate performance with specific awful symptoms
jokes.explanation: Great payoff using established character information. Freddy's commitment to the bit is impressive.

jokes.text: And you'll have to hold my penis when I pee. - Ay, gevalt.
jokes.setup: Freddy describing his fake disease symptoms
jokes.punchline: The intimate care detail that breaks Irma's resolve
jokes.explanation: Perfect breaking point detail. Irma's Yiddish exclamation shows this crossed her line completely.

jokes.text: You like it? Listen. Please, take it. Please, it's the least I can do. - Oh, please, no. - No, please. It's the least... - What? Really?
jokes.setup: Irma leaving after the Groat's disease revelation
jokes.punchline: Freddy immediately implements the 'I like it' gambit on his departing girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback execution. Using the gambit during a breakup is perfectly timed dark comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah! Gone!
jokes.setup: Irma finally leaves after the fake disease story
jokes.punchline: Freddy's triumphant celebration of freedom
jokes.explanation: Pure joy at the scheme's success. Good economy and character payoff.

jokes.text: Larry fucking David. Those fucking pricks.
jokes.setup: All the deceptions are revealed connecting back to Larry
jokes.punchline: The women realize Larry is behind all their problems
jokes.explanation: Great convergence payoff. Larry as the common denominator of everyone's problems is perfect Curb structure.

tmdb_overview: After an incident at temple, Larry asks friends to vouch for his character – while also trying to help name a baby and worrying about a fish.
